page: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose cloud server powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing a total of 64 vCPUs. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. The virtualization layer is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations indicate exceptional multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU scores all ranking in the top tier. The dedicated core architecture enables high-throughput operations, as evidenced by top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and software compilation using Clang. Memory bandwidth is also robust, with small-block cached read bandwidth placing in the top 10%, while large-block read and write operations remain strong. Single-core performance and database operations consistently rank in the top 25%, though single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average range.

From a resource density perspective, the b3-256 offers a highly balanced hardware profile. The absence of hyperthreading ensures that each of the 64 vCPUs corresponds to a dedicated physical core, maximizing compute predictability and eliminating noisy-neighbor CPU scheduling issues. This makes the server highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring sustained, predictable multi-core execution without the overhead of GPU acceleration. Recommended workloads include large-scale software compilation, multi-threaded data processing, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and relational database hosting.
description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server configured with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores operating at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 16 Gbps baseline network interface under a KVM hypervisor. Lacking hyperthreading, the instance provides dedicated compute resources per vCPU, resulting in top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and Redis transaction throughput. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, particularly for cached read operations. This balanced resource allocation makes the b3-256 a cost-effective solution for predictable, high-throughput workloads. It is highly suited for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, database hosting, and high-capacity web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for multi-core workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC server with balanced memory and fast NVMe storage for predictable, high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores with no hyperthreading for predictable compute performance
256 GB of system memory paired with a 400 GB local NVMe SSD
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and software compilation speeds
Well-suited for Redis caching, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing
